blocks.content: Through the wall — not words, but the register of a voice pushed past its limit. The specific sound of someone who has been holding themselves together by force of will for two days and finally couldn't anymore.
Not him. That's grief. That's someone who lost his closest friend and is being asked if he benefited from it. Those are not the same emotion.
The person who did this doesn't sound like that. The person who did this sounds like nothing — because they've had eight years to prepare for every question that gets asked in a room like that.

He knew Davenport would be asking hard questions. He knew some of those questions would land on the wrong person. That was the nature of this — you pressed everyone and the innocent ones cracked and the guilty one held because they had to.

I know who it is. I'm not wrong about this. But I can't say it — not yet, not without what I need — and so I have to sit here and listen to the wrong person break while the right one stays perfectly still.
He looked at his legal pad.
He left it turned over and went back to work.
